channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Elephants at San Diego safari park huddle to protect calves during earthquake
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — As the ground shook from a 5.2-magnitude earthquake, a herd of elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park sprung into action to protect their young.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FAA emergency order grounds NYC helicopter tour company involved in deadly crash
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal aviation regulators issued an emergency order Monday grounding the helicopter tour company involved in a deadly New York crash after learning it had fired its operations director minutes after he had agreed to suspend flights during the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tehran says next round of Iran-US nuclear talks in Oman after Rome named by officials
item.description: ROME (AP) — Talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program had appeared ready Monday to leave the Middle East, as an Italian source and others said the next round of negotiations would take place in Rome.
item.dc:creator: MARIA GRAZIA MURRU, JON GAMBRELL and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Alleged accomplice accused of helping disgraced lawyer Alex Murdaugh enters plea deal
item.description: The former banker and alleged co-conspirator of disgraced legal scion and convicted murderer Alex Murdaugh plead guilty to federal fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, after the institution said it would defy the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Salvadoran fugitive convicted in deadly attack of Maryland hiker
item.description: BEL AIR, Md. (AP) — A fugitive from El Salvador was convicted Monday in the 2023 slaying of a Maryland woman who was attacked while exercising on a popular hiking trail northeast of Baltimore.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Former Colorado deputy gets 3 years in prison for fatally shooting man who called for help
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of killing a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help was sentenced Monday to three years in prison, the maximum sentence, by a judge who said the shooting was about power.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southern Utah fire chief's trespass charge dismissed after donation, apology letter
item.description: Hurricane Fire Chief Joe Decker's charge was dismissed after he submitted a letter of apology and made a financial donation.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Video shows mother pick up baby and run during Southern California earthquake
item.description: A quick-thinking San Diego County mother saved her baby from a 5.2 magnitude earthquake in eastern San Diego County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Seven US service members had ‘COVID-19-like symptoms’ after 2019 Wuhan games: Pentagon report
item.description: Seven U.S. service members exhibited “COVID-19-like symptoms” during or after their return from the 2019 World Military Games in Wuhan, China, according to a Pentagon report recently made public.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KREX 5

item.title: Best and worst airlines for 2025 ranked, according to WalletHub
item.description: A new study compared the nine largest U.S. airlines and two regional carriers to help travelers decide which company suits them best.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Judge denies bail for man accused of setting fire at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's residence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man who allegedly scaled an iron security fence in the middle of the night, eluded police and set fire to the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion was denied bail Monday as he faced charges including attempted homicide, terrorism and arson.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Massive bluefin tuna catches halted at pier after officials step in, manager says
item.description: For several days a huge bluefin tuna were caught at Jennette's Pier in Nags Head, but the bluefin fun came to an end after an official visited last week, the pier said.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: El Salvador President Bukele says he won't be releasing a Maryland man back to the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's top advisers and Nayib Bukele, the president of El Salvador, said Monday that they have no basis for the small Central American nation to return a Maryland man who was wrongly deported there last month.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM and MARCOS ALEMÁN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Massive North Carolina bluefin tuna pier fishing halted after wild run
item.description: "Not every day you see someone catch a bluefin tuna off the pier!!" said Dauses, of Newport News, Virginia.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, NewsNation

item.title: FEMA denies Washington state disaster relief from bomb cyclone, governor says
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency has denied Washington state's request for emergency relief funds to help repair an estimated $34 million in damage from a deadly bomb cyclone storm system in November, according to Gov. Bob Ferguson.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance fumbles Ohio State's NCAA football championship trophy as Buckeyes visit the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President JD Vance ended the Ohio State football team's visit to the White House on Monday by fumbling the team's national championship trophy.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Self-driving Tesla hits car, sign while driver sleeps behind wheel
item.description: According to police, the driver was asleep at the wheel and using Tesla's self-driving feature to navigate.
item.dc:creator: Drew Kolek, NewsNation

item.title: AG: Man charged for AI-generated child sex material
item.description: “This proactive investigation uncovered a defendant weaponizing modern technology to victimize and harm children,” Attorney General said.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Thompson, NewsNation

item.title: Stocks rally worldwide after Trump eases some of his tariffs on electronics, for now
item.description: Stocks rose worldwide Monday after President Donald Trump relaxed some of his tariffs, for now at least, and as stress from within the U.S. bond market seems to be easing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Yankees' Aaron Judge to be US captain at 2026 World Baseball Classic
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Appointed U.S. captain for next year's World Baseball Classic, Aaron Judge will be playing for more than himself and his teammates. “It’s something special getting a chance to represent our country,” the New York Yankees star said after manager Mark DeRosa's announcement Monday.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department charges man with arson at New Mexico Tesla dealership and GOP headquarters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has charged a man with vandalizing a Tesla dealership in New Mexico and setting fire to the headquarters of the state Republican Party, according to court records unsealed Monday. A criminal complaint charges Jamison R.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A Palestinian activist expecting a US citizenship interview is arrested instead by ICE in Vermont
item.description: A Palestinian man who led protests against the war in Gaza as a student at Columbia University was arrested Monday at a Vermont immigration office where he expected to be interviewed about finalizing his U.S. citizenship, his attorneys said.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House launches national security investigation into pharma, semiconductors
item.description: The Trump administration disclosed Monday that it had opened an investigation into the effects on national security of importing certain pharmaceuticals, a move widely seen as the prelude to initiating tariffs on prescription drugs.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICE, ATF capture fugitive in multinational manhunt wanted for 'brutal murder' in the Caribbean
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement announced the capture of a fugitive, wanted in connection to a 2020 murder, who escaped custody twice in the Caribbean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, Fox 5

item.title: A celestial 'smiley face' will be visible to stargazers later this month: What to know
item.description: Stargazers who turn their eyes skyward — and use a little bit of imagination — will have a chance to spot a rare "smiley face" in space later this month.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, Fox 5

item.title: Diddy pleads not guilty to additional charges in new indictment
item.description: The music mogul faces federal charges of racketeering, sex trafficking and of transportation to engage in prostitution.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KRON4

item.title: Yellen sees 'very worrisome' pattern in Treasuries selloff
item.description: Former Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen has expressed concern over the economic uncertainty caused by President Trump's tariff policies, which she believes has led to a "really, very worrisome" trend for dollar-based assets.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KRON4

item.title: Reported intruder arrested near UnitedHealthcare headquarters months after CEO's killing
item.description: Police in the Minneapolis suburb of Minnetonka said the suspect was arrested outside of the United Healthcare corporate campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: These abortion abolitionists want women who get abortion to face criminal charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As Kristan Hawkins, president of the national anti-abortion group Students for Life, tours college campuses, she has grown accustomed to counterprotests from abortion rights activists.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump, Bukele say they won't return mistakenly deported man to US
item.description: Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele said Monday that he has no plans to return a Maryland man wrongfully deported to a prison in his country, telling reporters, “Of course I’m not going to do it.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin teen plotted to kill Trump to start 'political revolution': documents
item.description: Nikita Casap, a 17-year-old accused of killing his mother and stepfather, also plotted to assassinate President Donald Trump, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Family of American found dead on Bahamas beach fears ‘evidence will disappear,’ pleads for help
item.description: Maryland man Dinari McAlmont's family is pleading for "international help" after the 23-year-old was found dead on a Bahamas beach on April 6 during a family vacation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KTLA 5
